description: vec_map / vec_reduce / arithmetic over Vec(F64) etc. lower to Cranelift's vector IR. ~b[] and ~bits[] get their own specializations. The whole point of having vectors as a distinct type from lists.

comments.text: Depends on fz-ul4.11. Cranelift SIMD lowering for vec_map/vec_reduce needs Vec values reachable from JIT'd code, which means heap pointers across the JIT/interp boundary — that's the tagged-Value + heap-pointer-ABI work that moved into .11 alongside the GC. Could ship as interp-only SIMD with std::simd, but the ticket explicitly says "lower to Cranelift's vector IR".

title: SIMD codegen for vector ops
